questions.question: What is the definition of a clause according to the text?

options.A: A group of words without a subject
options.B: A group of words that forms part of a longer sentence and has its own subject and predicate
options.C: A single word that functions as a sentence
options.D: A phrase that does not contain a finite verb

questions.explanation: A clause is a group of words with its own subject and predicate, forming part of a complex or compound sentence.

questions.question: Identify the type of subordinate clause: 'Ajay remembers that it was a Friday.'
questions.explanation: The clause 'that it was a Friday' functions as the object of the verb 'remembers', making it a noun clause.

questions.question: What function does the noun clause perform in: 'What he says is correct'?
questions.explanation: The clause 'What he says' is the subject of the verb 'is'.

questions.question: In the sentence 'I asked Radha how she felt', 'how she felt' is a noun clause acting as:
questions.explanation: The clause serves as the direct object of the transitive verb 'asked'.

questions.question: Identify the noun clause function: 'The problem is that we do not have any money.'
questions.explanation: The clause 'that we do not have any money' completes the meaning of the linking verb 'is'.

questions.question: Which of these is a noun clause acting as an object of a preposition?

options.B: Pay attention to what I say.
options.C: That he is honest is true.
options.D: The news that he died is shocking.

questions.explanation: The clause 'what I say' follows the preposition 'to' and acts as its object.

questions.question: Identify the appositive noun clause: 'The news that his father has come is true.'
questions.explanation: The clause 'that his father has come' explains the noun phrase 'The news'.

questions.question: Relative clauses are also known as:
questions.explanation: Relative clauses function like adjectives by qualifying a noun or pronoun.

questions.question: Which relative pronoun is used for people in a defining relative clause?
questions.explanation: 'Who' is the relative pronoun used to refer to persons.

questions.question: In 'The book which is lying on the table is mine', what is the antecedent of 'which'?
questions.explanation: The antecedent is the noun that the relative clause describes, which is 'The book'.

questions.question: When can a relative pronoun be dropped from a sentence?

options.A: When it is the subject of the relative clause
options.B: When it is the object of the verb in the relative clause

questions.explanation: If the relative pronoun functions as the object in its clause, it can remain 'hidden' or be dropped.

questions.question: Which of these sentences allows the relative pronoun to be dropped?

options.A: The boy who is sitting there is my brother.
options.B: I have read the book which you gave me.
options.C: The man who stole the purse ran away.
options.D: Amit, who often comes here, is my friend.

questions.explanation: In sentence B, 'which' is the object of 'gave' and can be dropped ('the book you gave me').

questions.question: What is a characteristic of a non-defining relative clause?

options.A: It is essential to identify the noun.
options.B: It is separated from the main clause by commas.
options.C: It can start with the pronoun 'that'.
options.D: The relative pronoun can be dropped.

questions.explanation: Non-defining clauses provide extra information and are punctuated with commas.

questions.question: Which relative pronoun is NOT allowed in non-defining relative clauses?
questions.explanation: The text states that non-defining clauses cannot take the relative pronoun 'that'.

questions.question: Identify the non-defining relative clause: 'Pramod, who is my classmate, is very sincere.'
questions.explanation: This clause adds extra info about Pramod and is set off by commas.

questions.question: Choose the correctly punctuated sentence:

options.A: My father who is a teacher loves to talk.
options.B: My father, who is a teacher loves to talk.
options.C: My father who is a teacher, loves to talk.
options.D: My father, who is a teacher, loves to talk.

questions.explanation: Since 'father' is a known identity, the relative clause is non-defining and needs commas on both sides.

questions.question: In Activity 5, sentence viii: 'Uneasy lies the head that wears a crown.' This relative clause is:
questions.explanation: The clause 'that wears a crown' identifies which 'head' is being discussed.

questions.question: Which relative pronoun refers to 'possession'?
questions.explanation: 'Whose' is used to show a relationship of possession or belonging.

questions.question: Complete the proverb: 'All ________ glitters is not gold.'
questions.explanation: The standard proverb uses 'that' as the relative pronoun for 'All'.

questions.question: A defining relative clause is ________ to the meaning of the sentence.
questions.explanation: Defining clauses are essential because they identify the specific person or thing.

questions.question: Identify the noun clause function: 'Shanti didn’t know that her uncle had come.'
questions.explanation: It is the object of the verb 'know'.

questions.question: Noun clauses usually begin with which words?
questions.explanation: Noun clauses are typically introduced by wh-words, 'if', 'whether', or the conjunction 'that'.

questions.question: Identify the relative clause: 'The chair which has a cracked leg is in the corner.'

options.A: The chair is in the corner

questions.explanation: This clause provides information about 'the chair'.

questions.question: Which sentence contains a non-defining relative clause qualifying the whole main clause?

options.A: I found the book which I lost.
options.B: He has stopped attending classes, which is unfortunate.
options.C: The girl who won the prize is happy.
options.D: This is the scientist whose invention is famous.

questions.explanation: In this case, 'which' refers to the entire fact that he stopped attending classes.

questions.question: Rewrite using a relative clause: 'I write with a pen. My father bought it.'

options.A: I write with a pen which my father bought.
options.B: I write with a pen who my father bought.
options.C: I write with a pen my father bought it.
options.D: My father bought a pen I write.

questions.explanation: 'Which' is the correct relative pronoun for an inanimate object like a pen.

questions.question: In 'The rumor ________ is true', fill with a noun clause in apposition.
questions.explanation: 'That she has resigned' acts as an appositive noun clause explaining 'the rumor'.

questions.question: Relative clauses that describe 'place' begin with:
questions.explanation: 'Where' is the relative pronoun/adverb for locations.

questions.question: Relative clauses that describe 'time' begin with:
questions.explanation: 'When' is used for time-related antecedents.

questions.question: What is the function of the noun clause in: 'It is a pity that he doesn't come home.'
questions.explanation: The clause 'that he doesn't come home' is in apposition to the dummy subject 'It'.

questions.question: Which of these cannot be used as the subject of a passive construction but works in an active relative sentence?
questions.explanation: Reciprocal pronouns like 'each other' cannot be the subject of a passive clause.

questions.question: Identify the type: 'I cannot express how sorry I am.'
questions.explanation: It contains a main clause and a subordinate noun clause ('how sorry I am').

questions.question: In 'The movie depends on how it ends', the noun clause is:
questions.explanation: 'How it ends' is the noun clause acting as the object of the preposition 'on'.

questions.question: Defining relative clauses ________ commas.
questions.explanation: Defining clauses are not set off by commas because they are essential to the sentence.

questions.question: Identify the finite verb in the relative clause: 'The boy who always gives me books is my friend.'
questions.explanation: 'Gives' is the verb within the subordinate relative clause.

questions.question: Which sentence contains a noun clause as a complement?

options.A: He says that he is ill.
options.B: My decision is that he must help you.
options.D: That he is honest is clear.

questions.explanation: In B, the clause follows the linking verb 'is' and completes its meaning.

questions.question: A 'hidden' relative pronoun in the text: 'The house – which was the only one... – sat on the hill.' can be shortened to:

options.A: The house sat on the hill.
options.B: The house – the only one in the valley – sat on the hill.
options.C: The house which the only one.

questions.explanation: In the original text, the relative pronoun 'which' and the verb 'was' remain hidden.

questions.question: In 'The travellers who knew about the floods took another road', the relative clause is defining. This means:

options.B: Only the travellers who knew about the floods took another road.
options.C: No travellers knew about the floods.
options.D: The road was flooded for everyone.

questions.explanation: A defining clause restricts the meaning to a specific group.

questions.question: A conductor is a person ________.

options.B: who collects fares in a bus

questions.explanation: Relative clause definition based on Activity 3.

questions.question: A journalist is a person ________.
questions.explanation: Relative clause definition based on Activity 3.

questions.question: Which relative pronoun is used for 'belonging to a person'?
questions.explanation: 'Whose' is the possessive form of 'who'.

questions.question: Complete the phrase: 'The boy ________ we met at Konark is a tourist.'
questions.explanation: 'Whom' is the objective case of the relative pronoun for people.

questions.question: If a relative clause comes at the end of a sentence, we put a:
questions.explanation: According to the text, a full stop is used if the clause ends the sentence.

questions.question: Non-defining relative clauses cannot be used after ________.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that non-defining clauses cannot follow words like anyone, somebody, etc.

questions.question: What is the function: 'Tell me where you were born.'?
questions.explanation: The noun clause 'where you were born' is the direct object.

questions.question: In 'I understand how she felt', 'how she felt' is a:
questions.explanation: It functions as the object of the verb 'understand'.

questions.question: In 'The Curiosity which I felt... persisted', 'which' is a:
questions.explanation: It introduces a relative clause describing 'curiosity'.

questions.question: Which of these is a complex sentence?

options.A: The sun is shining and the day is warm.
options.B: The day is warm because the sun is shining.
options.C: The sun is bright and warm.

questions.explanation: Sentence B has one main clause and one subordinate adverb clause.

questions.question: Identify the type: 'What you told me is entirely false.'
questions.explanation: Contains a main clause ('is entirely false') and a noun clause subject.

questions.question: The marker 'whether' is typically used for:

options.B: Noun clauses with choices or Yes/No meanings

questions.explanation: 'Whether' introduces noun clauses, especially in subjects or objects involving alternatives.

questions.question: Relative clauses describing 'reason' usually begin with:
questions.explanation: 'Why' is the relative marker for reasons.

questions.question: Which sentence contains a noun clause in apposition to 'It'?

options.A: It is true that he is coming.
options.B: I asked him if it is true.

questions.explanation: The clause 'that he is coming' explains the dummy subject 'It'.

questions.question: Relative clauses follow ________.
questions.explanation: Relative clauses modify a preceding noun or pronoun (the antecedent).

questions.question: Complete the proverb: 'He ________ laughs last, laughs best.'
questions.explanation: Based on Activity 4 proverbs list.

questions.question: In 'The man that Mohan was talking to is his uncle', 'that' can be replaced by:
questions.explanation: Both 'who' and 'whom' are acceptable for people in objective relative clauses.

questions.question: Which relative pronoun is used for non-living things in defining clauses?
questions.explanation: Both 'which' and 'that' can be used for inanimate objects in defining clauses.

questions.question: What is the non-finite version of: 'He posted the letter after he had sealed it'?

options.A: He sealed the letter and posted it.
options.B: After sealing the letter, he posted it.
options.C: He posted the letter because it was sealed.

questions.explanation: Using the '-ing' form 'sealing' creates a non-finite clause.

questions.question: Which of these is a coordinate main clause?
questions.explanation: A clause joined by a coordinator like 'but' is a coordinate main clause.

questions.question: Which of these sentences is ungrammatical?
questions.explanation: Sentence B has no finite verb, making it incomplete/ungrammatical.

questions.question: Identify the subordinate clause: 'We thought that he was a hermit.'
questions.explanation: It is a noun clause subordinate to the main clause 'We thought'.

questions.question: Identify the type: 'A dog that barks seldom bites.'
questions.explanation: 'That barks' is a relative clause modifying 'A dog'.

questions.question: Identify the function: 'I have no objection to whatever you say.'
questions.explanation: 'Whatever you say' follows the preposition 'to'.

questions.question: Relative clauses are part of ________ sentences.
questions.explanation: Complex sentences consist of a main clause and at least one subordinate clause (like a relative clause).

questions.question: Relative pronouns that describe people in objective cases are:
questions.explanation: 'Whom' is the standard relative pronoun for people when they are the object.

questions.question: Which marker can start a noun clause showing a question meaning?
questions.explanation: Questions in reported speech or as noun clauses start with 'if', 'whether', or wh-words.

questions.question: Is 'Under the queen’s chair' a phrase, clause, or sentence?
questions.explanation: It is a group of words without a finite verb/subject-predicate structure.

questions.question: Identify the noun clause: 'It is feared that he will not help us.'

options.B: that he will not help us

questions.explanation: This is a noun clause in apposition to the subject 'It'.

questions.question: Defining relative clauses are used to ________ a large set.
questions.explanation: They help identify a specific item out of many.

questions.question: Non-defining clauses provide ________ information.
questions.explanation: The identity of the noun is already known; the clause just adds more details.

questions.question: A hospital is a place ________.
questions.explanation: Relative clause definition using the 'where' marker for place.

questions.question: Which sentence has a relative pronoun that CANNOT be dropped?

options.A: The pen which I bought is good.
options.B: The boy who won the prize is happy.
options.C: The book that you gave me is lost.
options.D: The dress which she is wearing is nice.

questions.explanation: In B, 'who' is the subject of the clause ('who won'). Subjects cannot be dropped.

questions.question: In 'All that glitters is not gold', 'that glitters' is a:
questions.explanation: It describes the pronoun 'All'.

questions.question: Which marker starts a clause of purpose?
questions.explanation: 'So that' introduces adverb clauses of purpose.

questions.question: In 'I like the man who has a smiling face', 'who' is the:
questions.explanation: It is the subject of the verb 'has'.

questions.question: Identify the type: 'Jack fell down and he broke his crown.'
questions.explanation: Two independent clauses joined by 'and'.

questions.question: Identify the noun clause function: 'The point is that he was absolutely honest.'
questions.explanation: It completes the verb 'is'.

questions.question: Relative pronouns ________ stay alone without an antecedent in relative clauses.
questions.explanation: Relative clauses must refer back to a noun or pronoun (the antecedent).

questions.question: The first word in a verb phrase that takes tense is called the ________ verb.
questions.explanation: Finite verbs carry tense and show agreement.

questions.question: A simple sentence has ________ clause(s).
questions.explanation: Simple sentences are defined by having only one finite clause.

questions.question: Identify the noun clause: 'When his father will return is uncertain.'
questions.explanation: This wh-clause functions as the subject of 'is'.

questions.question: Relative clauses in textbook Source 1 (I. Let's learn language) are identified as:
questions.explanation: Source 1 describes relative clauses starting with 'who' that give more info about characters like Lencho.
